description: UCGIS 2026 workshop reference KG — Meta-knowledge: AgentLoom architecture, schemas, propose-protocol

data.title: Data-Catalog UI Storytelling (8-section taxonomy)
data.description: Workshop attendees building catalog apps via propose-review need a shared template for what a catalog UI must communicate: provenance, acquisition, processing chain, KG-link, reuse — not just the data view. Without a documented + enforceable taxonomy, every catalog ships as 'pretty map, no story'. This knowledge node documents the 8 visible sections and design principles that all catalog UIs must follow.

data.title: Schema.org Dataset JSON-LD for catalog discoverability
data.description: The visible storytelling sections answer human questions; Schema.org Dataset JSON-LD answers machine questions (Google Dataset Search, federated catalogs, FAIR registries). Embedding a valid Dataset JSON-LD block in catalog HTML is the canonical way to make data findable on the open web. Standalone from the visible-section discipline so future catalogs can adopt JSON-LD without forcing the full UI taxonomy.

data.title: Canonical system prompt for the AgentLoom builder agent
data.description: First-class KG node containing the verbatim system prompt loaded by agentloom.builder_agent at startup. Making the prompt a versioned KG node — rather than a string literal in code — is what allows multiple host integrations (the standalone Python agent, Cline, Cursor, CI batches, future MCP-based hosts) to share one canonical specification of agent behavior; conformance becomes structural rather than aspirational. Also provides a stable reference point for paper-grade reproducibility (run logs pin a prompt_version, so any reported result can be re-derived against the exact prompt that produced it). Authoring this node is the LAST hand-driven propose; from this point forward the agent itself drives propose actions.
